title: ComfyUI Apply Signal
description: Written to comfyai/apply-patch-trigger.json to trigger the extension. Change 'ts' on every write to ensure the file watcher fires even if other fields are unchanged.

ts.description: Any monotonically increasing integer. Must change on every write — this is what triggers the file watcher. Seconds, milliseconds, or any counter all work. Example: Date.now() in JS, int(time.time()) in Python, or just increment a counter.

patchPath.description: Path to a comfyai/workflow-patch.json file. Relative paths resolve from the workspace root. The patch is merged into the current graph state. Mutually exclusive with sourcePath and command.

sourcePath.description: Path to a full ComfyUI workflow JSON file to load wholesale. Mutually exclusive with patchPath and command.

command.description: Command to execute. 'queue' submits the current workflow for generation; 'interrupt' stops the running generation; 'auto-layout' arranges all nodes left-to-right (note: groups are not repositioned); 'testing-mode' enables/disables testing reminders in apply-response.json. Mutually exclusive with patchPath and sourcePath.

logPath.description: Used with command 'testing-mode'. Path to the log directory (e.g. 'feedback/test4'). Each apply-response.json will include a log_file field naming a new per-action file to write (e.g. 'feedback/test4/log-1001.md'), plus a testing_reminder prompting comprehensive logging.

enabled.description: Used with command 'testing-mode'. Set to false to disable testing mode and stop reminders. Omit or set to true when enabling.
